sentences.text: The EU is accused of being complicit in the violent and illegal pushing of migrants  EU external border in the Balkans.
Information has visited one of the camps in Bosnia where  migrants end up.
Experts fear it's just a matter of time before people start to  die.
Open the borders!
Now we really can not stand it anymore, "shouts person.
The 26-year-old Afghan stands on a bench and throws a fist in the air in front of a freezing assembly.
Several shake as they stand with bare feet in sandals and rock flaps on the muddy ground that is  covered by the first snow of the year in northwestern Bosnia.
Their breath is shot away like raging clouds in it  frozen air as they roar in chorus at organisation call: ‘If person is willing, we will come to Italy.
If person is  willing, we come to Europe.
"They are probably already in Europe.
But not in the part they want to be in.
IN  instead, they are blocked on the wrong side of the organisation outer border, in the organisation tent camp, where the Bosnian police  have left them.
It is a camp without structure, without the most basic facilities and without leadership.
It is located on top of one  landfill .
On one side of the camp are the mountains leading to the EU country Croatia.
On the other is one  minefield from the 1990s Balkan wars, which have not yet been cleared.
And all the way around the camp is  the area transformed into an open toilet for the refugees.
In the camp, the ten men sleep per.
tent on carpets that are either damp or drenched.
"Here we are all animals just trying to survive," says person, who has so far spent two years of his  life on the trip from Pakistan to Europe.
He is about to start a fire to keep warm and get his shoes dried.
Fuel is plastic bags,  plastic plates and plastic packaging.
Once he has started a yellowish, poisonous fire, he slowly begins to put branches on.
But they're too wet,  and the fire goes out.
The reason why person, along with thousands of other migrants, is stuck in Bosnia is high  degree that organisation has actually transformed organisation into an enclosure.

Because according to the organisation migrant organization organisation, tens of thousands of migrants have been allowed to travel since January last year  unhindered through and out of organisation from Greece and up towards the Balkans and Bosnia.
But when they then try to continue their journey further north and into the union again, then another holds  EU country, Croatia, the gate to the fence closed.

And even when migrants succeed in crossing the border and  move deep into Croatia, they do not get the chance to apply for asylum in violation of EU law - instead,  they again and again violently beaten back to Bosnia by the Croatian police.
Thus, poor Bosnia has been left with the responsibility of dealing with migrants who otherwise just wanted to  pass through.
This has led to a situation that aid organizations describe as 'scandalous'.
person there  Auweraert, head of the organisation migrant organization organisation, says that “it is only a matter of  time before people begin to die ”of cold in the Vucjak camp.
After an evening and night of snow, which with its weight has caused several of the tents to collapse, the migrants in  Vucjak became extra desperate.
They want away.
Now .
And preferably across the border into Croatia.
If it is not possible ,  so to some of the organisation refugee camps in the area, but they are already full and have closed their doors.
That is why they want to go on hunger strike, "until Europe opens its borders," they say.
They will not accept the food that organisation delivers daily to the approximately 600 men here - far they  most are Afghans and Pakistanis.
"This is hell on earth," says person from organisation about the camp.
‘And it is inconceivable that one  such a place exists right here in Europe.
“The Finnish envoy finds it appalling that organisation is not making a major  efforts to tackle the 'humanitarian catastrophe' close to the Union's borders.
»It's easy to sit up in  Europe and make excuses, but when you stand and see this, you get angry, "he says.
Criticism of the conditions in the camp has been taken to recent weeks.
On a visit to the camp, organisation organisation, person, tells organisation that she, as a Bosnian and a European, 'is ashamed'  over the camp, which she describes as ‘unsuitable for humans’.
‘I’ve never seen anything like it in all of mine  life, ”she says.
»organisation should be shut down immediately.
"The criticism immediately seemed to have impressed the Bosnians  authorities, which after the organisation visit to the Vucjak camp declared that it should be closed.
The closure is  however, was later postponed indefinitely, allegedly because the authorities do not know where the migrants in  the place must be.

Until a few years ago, Bosnia was not part of the so-called Balkan route towards the organisation for refugees, but after  Hungary hermetically closed its border, Bosnia has become a hub for migrant flows.
According to organisation, 52,000 refugees and migrants have come to Bosnia since January last year, and the majority  passing through the area around the town of organisation.
Many live here in official camps, which are funded by organisation.
But  some thousands have sought refuge in disused factories and abandoned houses in the area.
And in May, police began to  force migrants out to the Vucjak camp to get them off the street scene in organisation.
Since then, thousands have passed  through the camp.
Violent border  From here, they make their attempt to reach organisation.
The 24-year-old Indian person points up towards the mountains,  the day before the snow announces its arrival.
You have to go uphill for eight hours, then you are at the border, he says.
‘My future is on the other side of  the mountains .
It is not here, 'he states.
person has unsuccessfully taken the trip seven times and has twice reached all the way to Slovenia.
But both the Slovenian and Croatian police have forced him back into Bosnia.

The two dozen refugees that organisation has interviewed in the Vucjak camp tell the same story as  Jangsher Singh: Although they manage to get into Croatia and sometimes even into Slovenia, so  in violation of organisation rules and international law and refugee conventions, they are forced - often violently -  back across the border into Bosnia without being given the opportunity to apply for asylum.
The many concurring testimonies thus point in the direction of a violent and illegal defense of organisation's external border, which the Croatian police have put in place.
26-year-old person from Lahore, Pakistan still has a swollen nose and the remnants of a blue eye after that,  he says the meeting with a Croatian police staff was small two weeks before.
‘They caught us and took all our stuff.
Our money, clothes and telephone.

They left us in underpants and told us to turn around, "he says as he warms tea and says  even up by the fire that has been lit up in a metal wheelbarrow without wheels and handles, and which he has placed  at the entrance to his tent.
He has now received a new set of clothes from organisation - apart from footwear.
‘I will try the trip again soon, but I have  need a new pair of shoes first, ”he says.
organisation, 25, from Afghanistan, says he has tried to cross the border five times.
He returned to camp after his most recent failed attempt just two days ago.
"They hit us with sticks, kick us and hit us," he said of the Croatian police.
‘They took all our stuff and  burned it all in front of us on a bonfire, and then they sent us back on bare toes and only in underpants and organisation organisation, ”says person.
“They said we should never come back again.
“Other refugees show wounds and broken fingers as they  says they got when they tried to protect themselves from the blows from Croatian police sticks.

organisation responsibility  Médecins Sans Frontières states that 'border guards in Croatia seem to be pushing people back across the border  more aggressively than before ', and this has led to a marked increase in the number of injuries suffered by the refugees  suffers from, among other things, broken bones and dog bites.
According to organisation organisation, 'hundreds, if not thousands, of migrants and asylum seekers' have become  abused by Croatian border police.
organisation has stated that the Croatian police "clearly violate the human rights of migrants".
Moreover  organisation has accused organisation of being 'complicit' in the Croatian police's systematic,  illegal and often violent expulsion 'from the country by thousands of asylum seekers.

The human rights organization says in a report that organisation countries prioritize border control over  the maintenance of international law, and therefore they are closing their eyes to the 'brutal abuses' of the Croatian police;  on migrants.

'European leaders can no longer ignore their responsibility for the continuing violent displacement along  The Balkan route, which is the result of their determined efforts to fortify organisation borders, no matter what they  human costs may be, ”said person, research manager for organisation in Europe, in  in connection with the publication of the report.
Croatia wants to be incorporated into the Schengen area and is therefore under pressure from Brussels to show that  the country is able to secure the external border of the organisation.

organisation sees the Croats' treatment of migrants as a consequence of organisation wanting to  deter potential migrants: 'It seems to be part of the European mindset that we are  need to send a clear signal to these people that we have closed the border and they are not welcome, "  says person.
»Both sides are hoping for their miracle.
organisation hopes that the migrants simply disappear while the migrants  hopes that organisation will open the borders.
None of them will get their wish fulfilled.

Purposeless abuse  Croatian police and politicians deny allegations of violence, but Croatian President organisationadmitted in a television interview earlier this year that police were forced to use 'a small  bit 'violence to deal with the illegal migrants.
However, it seems that the mistreatment of migrants on the border between Croatia and Bosnia is ultimately  utterly pointless.
For the migrants keep coming anyway - and the majority of them are ultimately successful  entering the EU.

person where organisation from organisation presents the accounts: Out of the 52,000 that have arrived at organisation since January last year, 43,000 have succeeded in crossing the organisation outer border.
"People sometimes have to try five or seven times, but as long as there is a chance, they will keep coming here," he says.
person the organisation.
No way back  The question, however, is what will happen to the remaining migrants in the country - especially those in the Vucjak camp.
There is no space in the already established refugee centers.
organisation has given Bosnia DKK 34 million.
euros since 2018 to  to deal with the refugee crisis, and the organisation says that there are sufficient funds to set up new centers, but  this has not happened due to political slowness in Bosnia.
The Bira camp in organisation, which is run by organisation and paid for by organisation, currently houses just under 1,600 migrants.
Among them are 340 unaccompanied minors.
The camp is located inside a former refrigerator factory, and the migrants sleep in containers located on organisation and row in the huge factory halls.
The head of the center, person, says that the number of refugees "has overwhelmed us".
Most  here in the Bira camp also makes attempts to get to organisation.
“When they leave, I always hope I never see them again, because that would probably mean that it is  managed to reach them, "says person.
Back in the Vucjak camp, several get ready to leave.

One of the camp's unaccompanied minors, 14-year-old person, will be with a small group  take a few steps back and go to the Bosnian capital, Sarajevo, to spend the winter there,  before resuming the journey towards organisation in the spring.
"If we stay here, we will just die," said the boy, who left his village near Kabul at the age of 12 in  Afghanistan to reach Europe.
However, other groups will try to cross the mountains towards Croatia one last time before it gets too cold.
Among them is 32-year-old person from Pakistan, who has already tried the trip no less than 14 times.
“Every time I get stopped by the police and sent back here, I think I have to give up and take  home to my two children in Lahore, but that's not an option.
I've already spent too much money on that  come here so everything will be in vain if I stop now.
There is no going back.
mago@information.dk  I've never seen anything like organisation.
